relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: A steward is accused of squandering what was entrusted.
Accountability comes for stewardship.
The steward faces loss of position and begins to reason.
He seeks future welcome through present action.
He calls the debtors one by one.
Oil debt is reduced quickly.
Wheat debt is reduced too.
Shrewdness exposes how this age knows how to act for survival.
Mammon must be converted into mercy-shaped relationship before it fails.
Trustworthiness in little reveals trustworthiness in much.
Untrustworthy handling of Mammon blocks true entrusting.
Faithfulness with another’s things prepares one’s own.
No one can serve both the Divine and Mammon.
Lovers of silver ridicule the teaching.
Human self-justification is transparent before the Divine heart-knower.
Law and Prophets lead to John; now the Divine reign is announced.
The Law is not casually erased.
Covenant betrayal is treated seriously.
A rich man lives in purple luxury every day.
Lazarus lies poor and wounded at the gate.
He longs for table crumbs while dogs tend his wounds.
The poor man is carried to Abraham, while the rich man is buried.
From the depths, the rich man sees Lazarus comforted.
He asks mercy through Lazarus, still treating Lazarus as messenger.
Abraham names the reversal of comfort and anguish.
The chasm fixed in life becomes visible after.
He asks for warning to his father’s house.
He wants his brothers spared the same end.
Moses and the Prophets are already enough witness.
He imagines a messenger from the dead would create turning.
If Moses and the Prophets are refused, even resurrection will not persuade.
